pattern: Binary search walks uphill: comparing the midpoint with its right neighbor reveals a slope that must lead to a peak, halving the search each step.
hints: Because the array's ends act like negative infinity, an upward slope always continues to some peak.
If the midpoint is lower than its right neighbor, a peak lies to the right; otherwise a peak lies at the midpoint or to its left.
Run lo/hi binary search: when nums[mid] < nums[mid+1] set lo = mid+1, else hi = mid; lo and hi converge on a peak index.

edge_inputs.description: single element is its own peak
strictly increasing, peak at the end
strictly decreasing, peak at the front
two elements, larger one is the peak

stress.description: random array with no equal adjacent values, exercising the uphill search
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    out = []
    prev = None
    for _ in range(n):
        v = rng.randrange(-10**6, 10**6)
        while prev is not None and v == prev:
            v = rng.randrange(-10**6, 10**6)
        out.append(v)
        prev = v
    return [out]
stress.note: no two adjacent values equal, as the constraints require
